title: An Effective American Technology Strategy
content:
The United States has long dismissed the Chinese system as being too rigid to foster true frontier innovation. The American democratic and capitalist model has undeniably produced a dazzling lineup of transformative technological breakthroughs over the years. However, while the U.S. cannot and should not replicate China's approach, it must find its own way to compete on a broader global playing field. Americans need to focus not only on innovating but also on producing advanced technology and controlling vital supply chain inputs. To achieve this, an effective American technology strategy must establish and protect enduring structural advantages that deliver concrete results.

First, the United States must revitalize its techno-industrial base to preserve its position at the cutting edge of global innovation. This requires generating enough domestic capacity to produce advanced technologies at scale across diversified and resilient supply chains. Simultaneously, the U.S. military must prioritize rapid adaptation, which is critical for deterring aggression and maintaining peace in vulnerable regions. Washington should also work to build a democratic digital order that establishes American technology as the prevailing global model. This model should be protected by high safety standards, financed transparently, and deeply rooted in respect for human rights and data privacy.

Finally, the United States must establish a baseline of stability in its relationship with China to prevent a destructive race to the bottom. Meaningful cooperation between the two technological superpowers is essential for addressing shared global challenges. These strategic goals are mutually reinforcing, as a strong industrial base directly supports both military power and economic security. By adopting this comprehensive approach, America can maintain its technological leadership without compromising its core democratic values. Ultimately, securing these high grounds will ensure that technological policy positively impacts the real lives of the American people.
